𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗙𝗔𝗡𝗜𝗧𝗬-𝗙𝗜𝗟𝗧𝗘𝗥𝗜𝗡𝗚-𝗔𝗟𝗟-𝗟𝗔𝗡𝗚𝗨𝗔𝗚𝗘𝗦 | To filter out or censor profane language in text across a wide range of languages for applications like chat platforms, content moderation, or user-generated content.  

▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 📘 TypeScript 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To filter out or censor profane language in text across a wide range of languages for applications like chat platforms, content moderation, or user-generated content.
📅 Created: Feb 2026 • Updated: Mar 2026

▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ 107 languages support
✅ Blazing fast O(1) lookups
✅ Lazy loading of locale data
✅ Zero runtime dependencies
✅ Rich API (check, detect, censor, addWords, removeWords)
✅ TypeScript-first with type declarations
✅ ESM + CommonJS dual export
✅ Customizable (add/remove words, custom censor characters)


▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐ 16 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 0 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 0 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

This TypeScript library provides a highly performant profanity filtering solution supporting 107 languages. It offers features like lazy loading, zero runtime dependencies, and a rich API for checking, detecting, and censoring profanity, with options for customization.

▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/Wart050/Profanity-Filtering-All-languages

▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

𝗖𝗟𝗔𝗠𝗦𝗛𝗘𝗟𝗟 | Enabling laptop users to close their lid and use external monitors seamlessly on Linux, regardless of power connection or init system.  

⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🔨 C++ 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Enabling laptop users to close their lid and use external monitors seamlessly on Linux, regardless of power connection or init system.
📅 Created: Feb 2026 • Updated: Mar 2026

⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Implements clamshell mode on Linux
✅ Does not require AC power
✅ Independent of init systems
✅ Configurable suspend modes (freeze, suspend_to_ram, suspend_to_disk)
✅ Specific NVIDIA GPU suspend handling


⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐ 12 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 0 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 0 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Clamshell is a Linux daemon that enables clamshell mode, allowing users to close their laptop lid and continue working with external displays and peripherals without requiring AC power or a specific init system. It offers flexible suspend mode options and specific handling for NVIDIA GPUs.

⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/Hato1125/clamshell

⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬⚬

𝗦𝗦𝗛𝗕 | To provide a fun, interactive Tamagotchi-like experience within the user's terminal environment, enhancing engagement with command-line usage.  🔥 Super Hot!

⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 💻 Shell 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To provide a fun, interactive Tamagotchi-like experience within the user's terminal environment, enhancing engagement with command-line usage.
📅 Created: Mar 2026 • Updated: Mar 2026

⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Tamagotchi-style virtual pet in the terminal
✅ Persistent pet state
✅ ASCII art pet with animated expressions
✅ Four core stats: Hunger, Energy, Happiness, Health
✅ Background daemon for stat decay (systemd)
✅ Bash prompt integration
✅ Interactive mode
✅ Pet aging and mood system
✅ Blackjack game
✅ Command quips based on exit codes


⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐ 57 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 5 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 1 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

SSHB (SSH Buddy) is a terminal-based Tamagotchi-style virtual pet that lives in your command line. It requires regular care, with its stats decaying over time, and can be integrated into your bash prompt to display its status.

⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/C-GBL/sshb

⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

𝗞𝗨𝗚𝗘𝗟𝗔𝗨𝗗𝗜𝗢-𝗢𝗣𝗘𝗡 | Providing high-quality, open-source text-to-speech synthesis for a wide range of European languages, enabling developers and researchers to create natural-sounding speech for various applications.  📈 Growing!

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐍 Python 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Providing high-quality, open-source text-to-speech synthesis for a wide range of European languages, enabling developers and researchers to create natural-sounding speech for various applications.
📅 Created: Jan 2026 • Updated: Mar 2026

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Open-source text-to-speech for European languages
✅ AR + Diffusion architecture
✅ High-quality voice cloning capabilities
✅ Outperforms leading commercial TTS systems (e.g., ElevenLabs) in human preference benchmarks
✅ Trained on 200,000 hours of speech data from the YODAS2 dataset
✅ Supports diverse speaking styles including neutral, shouting, singing, and drunken voice


⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐ 207 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 32 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 15 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

KugelAudio is an open-source text-to-speech (TTS) system focused on European languages, leveraging an AR + Diffusion architecture. It aims to bridge the gap in high-quality TTS for underserved European languages, offering performance that rivals and surpasses leading commercial solutions.

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/Kugelaudio/kugelaudio-open

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

𝗗𝗔𝗡𝗚𝗘𝗥𝗭𝗢𝗡𝗘 | To safely convert untrusted documents into a secure PDF format, mitigating potential security risks from malicious content.  🔥 Super Hot!

✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐍 Python 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To safely convert untrusted documents into a secure PDF format, mitigating potential security risks from malicious content.
📅 Created: Jan 2020 • Updated: Mar 2026

✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Sandboxed document conversion
✅ Conversion to pixel-based PDFs
✅ Optional OCR for text layer
✅ PDF compression
✅ Support for various document and image formats
✅ Uses Docker/Podman for sandboxing
✅ gVisor for enhanced Linux sandboxing


✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 5.2K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 245 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 198 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Dangerzone is a security tool that converts potentially dangerous documents (PDFs, Office files, images) into safe, pixel-based PDFs. It achieves this by processing the documents within a sandboxed environment, converting them to raw pixel data, and then reconstructing them as new PDFs, thus neutralizing embedded threats.

✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/freedomofpress/dangerzone
